reason_for_decision: The article passes because it treats control room layout as an operational workflow and coordination problem rather than as a furniture or display-count discussion.
strengths: The article is strong on separating room-level layout logic from workstation-level screen zoning, which gives the topic a clear and useful frame.
It gives practical decision value by linking shared displays, sightlines, acoustics, and role adjacency to real escalation and handoff behavior.
The surge and failure testing section keeps the article grounded in operations rather than in design aesthetics.
summary_assessment: This is a strong applied knowledge-base article. It is clear, technically believable, and gives readers a practical framework for designing a multi-sensor control room around workflow and coordination.
